reason: Settled earnings claim flow is exercised by integration tests, but the settled-branch anchor is not attributed reliably under --ir-minimum.

Direct library branch on injected collateral math state; not a protocol-flow target.

endListing zeroes remaining amount when deactivating a non-sold-out listing, so inactive non-sold-out state cannot persist under valid flows.

buyFromSecondaryListing handles expired listings via early cleanup/return before secondary validation, so this guard is not reached in valid flow.
All external purchase paths validate non-zero amounts before payment collection, so zero-amount transfer-from is unreachable.
Callers gate transfer amounts with positive seller net/fees, so direct zero-amount safeTransfer is unreachable in valid flow.
Protocol fee routing only runs with positive protocol fee or penalty totals; zero-amount route is not reachable from valid external inputs.

Initialized collateral inside _fundCurrentBuffers cannot have lockedAt == 0 through normal flows.
Primary redemption payout is bounded by current investor liquidity and current redemption supply under valid protocol state, so the zero-or-overflow guard is only defensive.
Zero-amount primary redemption is exercised by integration tests, but the early guard is not attributed reliably under --ir-minimum coverage mapping.

enableProceeds(assetId) only sizes buffers against current base liquidity, so the post-purchase sufficiency check cannot be satisfied through a valid external flow before this branch executes.

Unprotected liquidation explicitly exercises the zero-benchmark shortfall path, but the early-return branch is not attributed reliably under --ir-minimum.
Coverable-base output is already bounded by protected exposure and immediate-proceeds offsets; exceeding current baseCollateral is only a defensive clamp under malformed state.
Recomputed covered-base clamp is exercised by replenishment preview tests but not mapped reliably under --ir-minimum.
Release recomputation is bounded by base collateral, so exceeding total collateral requires malformed state.
ERC1155 balance checks prevent transfer position underflow before the internal FIFO walker can leave a positive remainder.
ERC1155 burn balance checks prevent generic revenue-token burns from reaching the FIFO remainder revert under valid external flows.
